hero.title: Participate in GYNT 2025
hero.subtitle: Join the Georgian Young Naturalists' Tournament and represent your school in an inspiring scientific challenge.

rules.label: Age
rules.text: Under 17 years old (born on or after January 1, 2009).

rules.label: Team
rules.text: 6 members (students from public or private schools).

rules.label: Leadership
rules.text: Each team has 1 or 2 leaders and a captain.

format.title: Tournament Format (Science Battles)
format.subtitle: The tournament runs in 6 rounds. At each stage, teams take on the roles of:

roles.desc: Criticizes the work and moderates the discussion (4 min).
Evaluates the Reporter's and Opponent's performance (3 min).

problems.title: 2026 Problems (Short List)
problems.subtitle: The tournament's 17 problems include diverse experiments. Full English version at [iynt.org](https://www.iynt.org/).

list.title: 1. Singing Wine Glass
list.desc: Study the sound produced by a wet cloth on a plastic glass.

list.title: 2. Saliva
list.desc: Starch breakdown into sugars and study of fermentative activity.

list.title: 3. Blue Bottle
list.desc: Analysis of methylene blue color change cycles.

list.title: 7. Thermos
list.desc: Experimentally determine the rate and efficiency of water cooling.

list.title: 11. Hair Drying
list.desc: Use a microscope to verify whether shampoo damages hair.

list.title: 13–17. Invent
list.desc: Create your own problems on mathematical art, archaeological seriation, and 'sixth sense'.

evaluation.title: Evaluation and National Team Selection
evaluation.formula_desc: Scores are calculated using a formula that considers both individual and team results.
evaluation.selection_desc: Takes place within 1 week after the tournament. Candidates must have two problems ready and be able to conduct the discussion in English.

register.title: Ready to Register?
register.desc: Submit your school using the official registration form.

contact.title: Contact Information
contact.organizer: Ilia Vekua Physics-Mathematics Tbilisi Public School No. 42

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Can students from different schools form a team?
faq.a1: No, all team members must be from the same school.
faq.a2: Participants must be younger than 17 on January 1, 2026.
